titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Adelbert F. Whitcomb correspondences and photographs
Adelbert F. Whitcomb correspondences and photographs
titlestmt.author: by Dan Duncan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 7, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: Adelbert F. Whitcomb family correspondence and photographs

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondences and photographs, circa 1876-1927. The correspondences are between Montana Central Railway engineer Adelbert F. Whitcomb his mother, Palmyra E. Whitcomb and other family members documenting his travels. The photographs are of the surveying team and rail lines in the Montana Territory.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and photographic prints sent to Palmyra E. Whitcomb by family members predominately from her son, Adelbert Finney Whitcomb, detailing his life in late 19th through early 20th century America as a railway civil engineer for the Montana Central Railway, and as the chief engineer of the Western Division of the Great Northern headquarters in Spokane, Washington. During this time, Adelbert spent time being treated at several prominent wellness sanitariums throughout the United States, which included the Battle Creek Sanitarium run by Dr. John Harvey Kellogg in Battle Creek, Michigan, and The Jackson Sanitarium in Dansville, New York.

Many of the letters represent the elegant stationary from these sanitariums and hotels where Adelbert traveled and worked. There are only a few letters to family members from Adelbert to his father, Erastus, his sister Elizabeth Ann Hunter, referred in letters as Mrs. Daniel J. Hunter, and her son Claude Hunter.
The 5 X 8 mounted photographs are of the surveying team in the Montana Territory by landscape photographer James Wantz, of Helena Montana. Some photographs have duplicates.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Palmyra E. Whitcomb (1830-1913) and Erastus Whitcomb (1813-1882) had a large dairy farm in Richmond, Vermont. Their children were Montana Central Railway Engineer Adelbert Finney Whitcomb (1854-1930), Elizabeth Ann Whitcomb Hunter (1853-1928), Averintha Whitcomb Hodges (1856-1944), and Irving Barber Whitcomb (1859-1933).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Adelbert F. Whitcomb Correspondence and Photograph collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in two series, correspondence and photographs. The correspondence are arranged chronologically and the photographs are arranged be similar themes.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

did.unittitle: Adelbert to Palmyra and Ann Hunter
Adelbert to Palmyra and Ann Hunter
Adelbert to Palmyra, Elizabeth Ann, and Claude
Surveying tools by cabins and tents, with men, women, horse and carriage
Montana Central Railway Engineering Party by cabins and tent with Asian cook
